context.meal: breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce
context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop in Front

episode_text:
day=10; meal=breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce; setting=Working on laptop with laptop in Front; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to reheat cold fruit meals, consistent with intended serving temperature. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user again chose not to reheat a cold fruit meal, suggesting meal type matters more than social context for fruit. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN reheating is consistently preferred rather than serving the meal cold BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected 2 min or 3 min for savory entrées in work contexts. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the hottest observed reheating level in that left-laptop savory work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected strong reheating while working with a front-facing laptop. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user chose reheating for a savory meal in that social context. IF watching TV in front or to the right AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as chicken bites or nuggets with sides THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the hottest observed reheating level in multiple TV-viewing savory meals. Across observed meals, cold fruit stays unmicrowaved, while savory entrées are typically reheated, with 3 min common in TV-viewing and some work contexts.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize view preservation in the observed personal setting. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid blocking the social partner on the left. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid blocking the social partner on the right. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve the view of the TV. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user chose to preserve the front-facing laptop view in the new work meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize preserving the right-side TV view in that observed meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both minimize right occlusion and do not consider occlusion selected, BECAUSE the user treated right-side work visibility as important in one work meal but not in another. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve the left-side laptop view in that meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize left-side visibility in that other left-laptop work meal. Across observed meals, occlusion preference is direction-specific and context-specific rather than uniformly applied whenever a person or screen is present.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred a comfortable, unhurried motion style. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected the more comfortable and socially gentle motion style. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user again preferred a comfortable, socially gentle motion style. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN slow BECAUSE the user preferred a comfortable pace while attending to a front-facing screen. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a somewhat faster pace than in other TV and social meals, but not the fastest setting. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected a comfort-oriented pace even in a front-laptop work context. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN medium to fast, with both selected, BECAUSE the user often shifts toward efficiency in right-laptop work contexts. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN slow to medium, with both selected, BECAUSE the user chose medium in one left-laptop work meal but slow in another, indicating less consistent pressure for speed than in right-laptop work meals. Across observed contexts, personal and most social settings favor slow movement, while work settings can shift the user toward faster operation, especially on the right, but front-laptop work has so far remained slow.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this is the default higher-success acquisition style and no contrary fruit-specific preference has been established. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style in that meal. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets and broccoli meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style for that meal. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized savory items such as buffalo chicken bites or nuggets THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-success acquisition style in those meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style for that work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user again selected the higher-success acquisition style in that left-laptop savory meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user again selected the higher-success acquisition style in a front-laptop savory meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected a narrower bite-acquisition style for that food. Across observed meals, skewering preference appears food-dependent rather than driven by social or media context alone, with perpendicular generally favored except for the observed sandwich meal.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose to avoid extra confirmation overhead. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the user still chose to skip explicit bite-acquisition confirmation even in a social setting. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred not to add this extra interaction step in a social meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reduced interaction burden and greater automation. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred not to interrupt the meal with extra web confirmation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers not to add this extra web confirmation step.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's selected and physically appropriate transfer mode is inside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal readiness cues. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again chose multimodal signaling. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again selected multimodal readiness cues in a social meal. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal readiness cues in both observed TV-viewing meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal readiness cues in the observed front-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both speech and speech + LED selected, BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only in one efficiency-oriented work meal but chose multimodal cues in another. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal cues in the observed left-laptop meals. Across observed meals, speech + LED is the dominant pattern, with speech-only appearing as a work-context efficiency variant.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and explicitly preferred an active but low-effort readiness signal. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user preferred voluntary control despite the social setting. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred voluntary low-effort signaling in a social meal. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user preferred low-effort voluntary control rather than automatic initiation. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected a more automated initiation style in that TV-viewing meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary low-effort signaling in the observed front-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary low-effort signaling in both observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and open mouth selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers highly automated initiation in that work context but also sometimes retains voluntary control. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and the user generally favors open mouth, with autocontinue used selectively in more automation-tolerant contexts.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and selected a low-effort voluntary readiness cue. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred voluntary low-effort control. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred voluntary readiness signaling in a social meal. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user preferred voluntary readiness signaling. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic progression for sip initiation in that observed TV meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary readiness signaling in the observed front-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary readiness signaling in the observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and open mouth selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers automatic progression in that work context but also sometimes retains voluntary control. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and drinking initiation usually mirrors feeding: open mouth by default, with autocontinue used selectively in more automation-oriented contexts.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation in a social meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user preferred automatic progression for mouth-wiping initiation across observed work meals, including the front-laptop meal. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers higher automation for wiping initiation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal completion cues. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again chose multimodal completion signaling. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again selected multimodal completion cues in a social meal. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues in both observed TV-viewing meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues in the observed front-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both speech and speech + LED selected, BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only completion cues in one efficiency-oriented work meal but chose multimodal cues in another. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues in the observed left-laptop work meals. Across observed meals, speech + LED is the dominant pattern, with speech-only appearing as a work-context efficiency variant.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected the reliable low-effort completion detection method for feeding.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and previously selected perception-based completion detection for drinking. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the user previously selected perception-based drinking completion in social meals. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic drink-completion progression in that observed TV meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected explicit sensor-based completion rather than automatic progression in that work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perception BECAUSE the user again selected explicit sensor-based completion in that left-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected explicit sensor-based completion in the observed front-laptop work meal. Across observed meals, button use is physically infeasible; perception remains the default and most common choice, with autocontinue appearing as a context-specific automation preference in one TV-viewing meal.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and previously selected perception-based completion detection for mouth wiping. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the user previously selected perception-based wiping completion in social meals. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic wiping-completion progression in that observed TV meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected explicit sensor-based completion rather than automatic progression in that work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perception BECAUSE the user again selected explicit sensor-based completion in that left-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected explicit sensor-based completion in the observed front-laptop work meal. Across observed meals, button use is physically infeasible; perception remains the default and most common choice, with autocontinue appearing as a context-specific automation preference in one TV-viewing meal.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to add extra retraction motions. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the user still preferred to reduce meal time rather than retract between tasks. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred not to add extra retraction even in a social meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized efficiency over additional retraction, including in the front-laptop work meal. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly chose retraction while attending to a front-facing screen, consistent with preserving visibility in that context. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the user did not choose retraction in that right-TV meal. Across observed meals, the user usually prefers not to retract, with retraction appearing specifically when preserving a front-facing TV view is salient.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and not selected. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and the user did not select it. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup, tartar sauce, or another available dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE in observed work meals with available sauce the user explicitly chose not to add dipping. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal without a selected dipping sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user again chose no dipping. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ranch dressing or ketchup THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to dip despite sauce availability in multiple TV-viewing meals. Across observed meals, the user avoids dipping by default, with one observed exception of restrained dipping in a social setting.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred a longer pause before automatic continuation. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a longer wait, suggesting more tolerance for pauses in social interaction than in work-focused meals. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user again preferred a longer wait in a social meal. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user again preferred a longer wait rather than rapid automatic progression. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred rapid automatic continuation and a time-efficient workflow in right-laptop work settings. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both 10 sec and 100 sec selected, BECAUSE the user chose a short wait in one left-laptop work meal but a long wait in another, indicating less stable timing preference there. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a longer wait in the observed front-laptop work meal. Across observed contexts, the user prefers longer waits in personal, social, TV-viewing, and front-laptop work settings, with short waits concentrated in efficiency-oriented work contexts, especially on the right.
